questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: $9.65 \mathrm{C}$ of electric current is passed through fused anhydrous magnesium chloride. The magnesium metal thus, obtained is completely converted into a Grignard reagent. The number of moles of the Grignard reagent obtained is
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{MgCl}_{2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mg}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{Cl}^{-}$
$\mathrm{Mg}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \underset{1 \mathrm{~mol}}{\mathrm{Mg}}$ (at cathode)
$\because 2 \mathrm{~F}(2 \times 96500 \mathrm{C}) \text { deposits } \mathrm{Mg}=1 \mathrm{~mol}$
$\therefore 9.65 \mathrm{C}(2 \times 96500 \mathrm{C}$ ) deposits $\mathrm{Mg}=1 \mathrm{~mol}$
$=\frac{1 \times 9.65}{2 \times 96500}$
$=5 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~mol}$
In order to prepare Grignard reagent, one mole of $\mathrm{Mg}$ is used per mole of reagent obtained. Thus, by $5 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{Mg}, 5 \times 10^{-5}$ mole of Grignard reagent are obtained.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: When acetylene is passed through red hot iron tube, compound $X$ is formed. Which one of the following reactions will yield $X$ as the major product?
questions.solution:
When acetylene is passed through red hot iron tube, benzene is formed as
$3 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_2 \stackrel{\text { red hot tube }}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_6$$(X)$
Reaction (a) also gives $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_6$ (or $X$ ) as
$\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{OH}+\mathrm{Zn} \stackrel{\text { distillation }}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_6+\mathrm{ZnO}$

questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The ease of dehydrohalogenation of alkyl halide with alcoholic \(\mathrm{KOH}\) is
questions.solution: Hints : Such dehydrohalogenation follows \(\mathrm{E}_2\) mechanism. The driving force of such reactions is the stability of alkene produced. Since tetriary alkyl halide can give more substituted alkene, it reacts fastest followed by secondary and primary i.e. \(3^{\circ} > 2^{\circ} > 1^{\circ}\).
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Which of the following  will undergo easy dehydrohalogenation on reaction with alc. KOH?

I. 2-chloropropane

II. chloroethane

III. 2-chloro 2-methyl propane
questions.solution:
This problem includes includes conceptual mixing of dehydrohalogenation and stability of alkene according to Sytzeff's rule.
Student are advised to complete the elimination reaction first and then choose the correct stable product using Sytzeff's rule.
Dehydrohalogenation reaction
The Dehydrohalogenation reaction of alkyl halide occurs by removal of hydrogen and halogen from adjacent position.



Sytzeff's rule : According to Sytzeff's rule, the more substituted alkene is formed in major amount due to more stability.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Examine the structural formulas of the following compounds and find how many compounds will produce CO2 on oxidative ozonolysis:
questions.solution:
Ozonolysis of alkene and alkyne gives different compounds based on reductive ozonolysis and oxidative ozonolysis.

Oxidative ozonolysis of non-terminal alkenes gives carboxylic acid.

Oxidative ozonolysis of non-terminal alkenes gives ketone.

Oxidative ozonolysis of terminal alkyne gives carboxylic acid.

Similarly, oxidative cleavage of terminal alkyne gives carboxylic acid and carbon dioxide.

.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.solution:
The ring attached to the nitrogen atom in benzanilide is strongly activated towards electrophilic substitution reaction. $\therefore$ Nitration occurs at $p$-position to the ring attached to ' $\mathrm{N}^{\prime}$ atom.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The principle product of the following reaction is:
questions.solution:
Proceeds through bromonium ion formation, a cyclic bridged ion in presence of methanol.



Methoxide anion OCH 3 - as a nucleophile attacks in anti position from back side atC⊛ atom which can share positive charge of bridged cation in better fashion to give  as product.

Br and OCH3 must be trans to one another because CH3OH attacks from the anti-position to cyclic bromonium ion.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: In the reaction with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math>, an alkene reacts following the Markovnikov's rule to give the product <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo></math>chloro<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo></math>methylcyclohexane. The possible reaction of the alkene is
options: and
questions.solution: The Markovnikov's rule asserts that with the addition of a protic acid HX to an asymmetric alkene, the acid hydrogen (H) gets attached to the carbon with more hydrogen substituents and the halide (X) group gets attached to the carbon with more alkyl substituents.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: Find out isomers A and B ?
questions.solution:
Alkene on the ozonolysis formed the carbonyl compounds. The carbonyl compound formed by the alkenes are aldehyde and ketones.

So, the above alkenes are giving the same product on ozonolysis. These are the isomers of each other.

Hence, the isomer A and B are .
